title: Shabu Shabu Hotpot
description: Thinly sliced wagyu swirled through a rich dashi broth with mushrooms, noodles, and all the fixings. A whole night around the pot.
ingredients: 1 napa cabbage, cut into large pieces
Ponzu and sesame dipping sauces, to serve
instructions: Bring the dashi broth to a gentle simmer in a wide shallow pot set over a portable burner at the table.
Arrange the beef, tofu, mushrooms, cabbage, and noodles on plates around the pot so everyone can reach.
Each person swirls a slice of beef through the hot broth for 10–15 seconds until just cooked through.
Add vegetables and tofu directly to the pot and cook 2–3 minutes until tender. Replenish broth as needed throughout the meal.
Dip everything into ponzu or sesame sauce and eat as you go. End the meal by adding noodles to the remaining broth.
notes: Recipe to be filled in — we know how to make this one by heart.

title: Braised Beef Stew Over Rice
description: Fork-tender beef in a dark, spiced broth with a soft-boiled egg and cilantro. The kind of bowl you want on a cold night.

title: Japanese Golden Curry
description: S&B Golden Curry with beef, potatoes, carrots, and pearl onions. The box is not cheating — it's tradition.

title: Mapo Tofu with Garlic Spinach
description: Silky tofu in a bold, numbing ground pork sauce alongside garlicky stir-fried greens and rice. A perfect weeknight trio.

title: Pork Katsu with Tonkatsu Sauce
description: Panko-crusted pork cutlet fried golden, sliced and served over steamed rice with a dark, tangy tonkatsu sauce.
ingredients: 2 pork loin chops, about 3/4 inch thick
Tonkatsu sauce, steamed rice, and shredded cabbage to serve
instructions: Using the flat of a knife, pound the pork chops to about 1/2 inch thick. Season both sides generously with salt and pepper.
Set up a breading station: flour in one bowl, beaten eggs in another, and panko in a third.
Dredge each cutlet in flour, shaking off the excess. Dip in egg, then press firmly into the panko until fully coated on all sides.
Heat 1 inch of oil in a wide skillet to 350°F. Fry the cutlets 3–4 minutes per side until deep golden brown and cooked through.
Drain on a wire rack for 2 minutes, then slice into strips. Serve over steamed rice with shredded cabbage and a heavy pour of tonkatsu sauce.

title: Congee with Soft-Boiled Egg
description: Slow-cooked rice porridge with chicken, topped with a jammy soft-boiled egg, crispy garlic, and scallions.

title: Herb Roast Chicken with Tomato Pasta
description: A whole roast chicken with garlic, lemon, and thyme alongside a bright fresh tomato pasta. Sunday dinner energy.

title: Pan-Seared Salmon with Arugula Salad
description: Crispy-skinned salmon fillet with a peppery arugula salad, cherry tomatoes, and roasted cauliflower.

title: Banana Peanut Butter Toast
description: Toasted bread spread with peanut butter, topped with banana coins and a heavy dusting of cinnamon. Our go-to.

title: Bangladeshi Rice Plate
description: A full spread: crispy fried fish, golden okra bhaji, sautéed spinach, aloo curry, and white rice. Grandma-level.

title: Creamy Chicken Pasta Soup
description: Lasagna noodles in a rich, herby cream broth with butternut squash and mushrooms. Cozy doesn't begin to cover it.

title: Wagyu Gyudon with Raw Egg Yolk
description: Thinly sliced wagyu over steamed rice with a grilled shiitake and a raw yolk dropped right in the center.
instructions: Combine dashi, soy sauce, mirin, and sugar in a shallow pan. Bring to a simmer over medium heat.
Add the sliced shiitake mushrooms and cook 2 minutes until softened.
Lay the wagyu slices into the simmering broth in a single layer. Cook 30–45 seconds — just until the pink is gone. Do not overcook.
Spoon the beef, mushrooms, and broth generously over bowls of hot steamed rice.
Make a small well in the center of each bowl and carefully drop a raw yolk in. Serve immediately and stir before eating.

title: Chicken & Sausage Jambalaya
description: A cast-iron full of smoky andouille, chicken, tomatoes, celery, and long-grain rice. One-pot magic.

title: Seared NY Strip Steak
description: A thick strip steak seared to a perfect medium-rare with a salt crust, sliced and rested. Simple and stunning.

title: Mushroom Risotto
description: Creamy, loose risotto with mushrooms and mascarpone, finished with parsley. Stirred to order, not a second early.

title: Carne Asada Tacos
description: Charred skirt steak in flour tortillas with cilantro, white onion, lime, and Mexican rice on the side.

title: Roast Chicken Drumsticks with Mashed Potatoes
description: Golden, fall-off-the-bone drumsticks glazed in their own pan juices, served over buttery mashed potatoes.

title: Hainanese Chicken Rice
description: Poached whole chicken served with ginger-scented rice, a clear bone broth, dark soy, and chili sauce.

title: Full English Breakfast
description: Fried egg, baked beans, sausages, hash browns, mushrooms, and sourdough toast. All of it, on one plate.

title: Herb-Crusted Rack of Lamb
description: Lamb chops with a rosemary and garlic crust, roasted to a brilliant pink. Fancy enough for a birthday.

title: Campfire Beef Kebabs & Fish Ceviche
description: Grilled beef and veggie skewers alongside a fresh white fish ceviche with pineapple and red onion. Lakeside.

title: Steamed Blue Crabs with Onion Ceviche
description: Whole blue crabs steamed until sweet, served with a bright red onion and lemon ceviche alongside.

title: Pork Bone Soup with Steamed Clams
description: A delicate pork bone broth with tender pork belly alongside a bowl of steamed little neck clams.

title: Fireplace S'mores
description: A marshmallow torched to a deep golden-black over the fireplace, squished between graham crackers and a Hershey square.

title: Miso-Glazed Chicken Thighs over Rice
description: Bone-in chicken thighs caramelized under a miso glaze, served over rice with wasabi and Japanese mayo.

title: Malatang Spicy Hotpot
description: A fiery Sichuan-style split broth hotpot loaded with tofu, enoki, bok choy, and thinly sliced beef.

title: Ground Beef Tacos with Guacamole
description: Seasoned ground beef with pico, guac, and Mexican rice in flour tortillas. Taco Tuesday every day.

title: Suan Cai Chicken Soup
description: A tangy, warming soup made with pickled mustard greens, shredded chicken, and glass noodles.

title: Bangladeshi Biryani Spread
description: A full feast: fragrant biryani, chicken curry, and lentil dal with cilantro. The whole table disappears fast.

title: Silken Tofu with Century Egg
description: Cold silken tofu with sliced century eggs, a soy-sesame drizzle, and scallions. Deceptively elegant.

title: Chicken Curry
description: A deep, slow-cooked chicken curry with warm spices and cilantro, served over white rice.

title: Bangladeshi Feast — Crab, Biryani & Dal
description: Restaurant-style whole crab in dark spices, fried rice, and a bright lentil dal. A celebratory spread.

title: Camping Breakfast
description: Grilled kielbasa, fried eggs, and a rotini pasta salad on blue enamel plates. Tastes better outdoors.

title: Instant Pot Bulalo
description: Filipino beef shank and bone-marrow soup, made the weeknight way in the Instant Pot. Frozen shank goes straight in — no blanching — for a deeply savory, ginger-forward broth with tender meat, marrow, and napa cabbage.
ingredients: 1.5 lbs frozen beef shank with bone and marrow
A thumb or two of ginger, sliced (we use a lot)
2 tbsp fish sauce, plus more to taste
5–6 cups water, enough to cover the meat
½ head napa cabbage, cut into 3–4 inch chunks
Optional: 1 bunch bok choy, or corn and potatoes for a heartier pot
instructions: Load the still-frozen beef shank into the Instant Pot with the onion, peppercorns, ginger, rock salt, fish sauce, and 5–6 cups water — enough to cover the meat. No blanching: you can't blanch frozen meat, so the broth comes out a little cloudier but just as tasty.
Seal and pressure cook on High for 45 minutes — 10 minutes longer than usual to make up for the frozen start. Let the pressure release naturally for 15 minutes.
Open the lid and skim the fat and scum off the top of the broth with a ladle.
Add the napa cabbage (plus bok choy, corn, or potatoes if using) and switch to Sauté mode for 3–5 minutes, until the vegetables are tender.
Taste the broth and adjust with more fish sauce or salt.
Ladle into bowls, making sure each serving gets marrow, meat, and broth. Serve hot with rice and a dipping sauce of fish sauce, calamansi or lemon, and chilies.
